mentions.note: Developing Trainium and Trainium alternatives to reduce Nvidia chip reliance. If equity offering follows Google's, will dilute supply further just before SpaceX IPO.
May do a fundraiser per Cramer's speculation. Supply could hit stock if they tap capital markets.
Heavy data center capex similar to Microsoft; may need to raise equity to stay competitive. Not yet public on plans but could be forced to tap markets alongside Alphabet, Anthropic, SpaceX trio.

Notably absent from Jensen's client shout-outs despite owning Anthropic stake ($14B). Cramer notes competitive tension; in-house chip development challenges Jensen's stance but Amazon not defending position.

Misunderstood but now huge believer; cleaning up with Prime, AWS, advertising, and semiconductors (own chips $50B standalone business potential); new chips hold value similar to Nvidia but lower price; reason Nvidia stalled while Amazon climbed.
Hyperscale data center operator and AI cloud player; Nvidia reported 115% hyperscale revenue growth; Amazon positioned to benefit from infrastructure buildout.
FANG stock and Magnificent Seven member; borrowed heavily in early days but massive opportunity; secular growth with scalability.
High-quality growth name that performed well during recession; example of company that works in thick or thin economic cycles.

Andy Jassy noted in shareholder letter that Amazon actually lowered prices vs. last year; valuable FedEx customer (recent relationship); competing 3PL offering is non-asset play, not threat to FedEx true end-to-end network; win-win relationship.
Cloud/data center provider in infrastructure layer. Spending hundreds of billions on AI data center expansion and jobs.
AWS data center capex commitment is existential moat. CEO Andy Jassy: not spending enough means losing billion-dollar customers to Alphabet/Microsoft. Data center packed with paying customers.
Spending tens of billions now for hundreds of billions later. Data center capex ensures colossal multi-year profits. One of two main publicly traded winners in cloud race.
Ultimate trade-down play; computer-driven economy immunity to oil/rate shocks; dominates new economy with logistics, cloud, and supply chain services.
$200B AI capex investment transformational; supply chain, semiconductors, and satellites create new $15B+ revenue streams; prices falling despite inflation through operational excellence.
17% revenue growth, 75% EPS growth; AWS up 28% (best in 4 years); custom chips $20B run-rate; good numbers but stock ran ahead; parabolic consolidation.
AWS grew 28% insane growth; key division Andy Jassy leads now thriving. May need more power, compute for data center work.

Reporting Q1 earnings tomorrow (earnings D-Day); Cramer owns in trust; watch capex guidance vs. revenue growth — if excess capex without matching revenue, stock will get crushed; historically mega-cap tech reports coincide with market peaks.
Reports Wednesday with Alphabet, Meta, Microsoft; critical focus on AI capex spending — will they pause or accelerate? Stock direction depends on capex guidance.
Soaring post-Jassy vision letter; data center strength; may have best strategy on pullback; held in charitable trust.
Jassy letter explained business direction, satellite acquisition added momentum; feared Web Services slowdown and retail weakness not materializing. Ready to run.
Retail and tech conglomerate; Jennifer's position; benefits from data center, cloud growth alongside e-commerce; diversified revenue streams.

AWS on track for $161B revenue; $200B 2026 CapEx backed by pre-sold customer commitments; homegrown AI chips in high demand; Jassy letter confirms 4:1 return potential. Cramer tempted to add despite low cost basis.
Weeks of bear whispers about consumer slowdown hurting Prime; Cramer knows Amazon is 'tight as a drum' with no leaks; didn't panic sell; company discipline and secrecy is strength.
Okay day but must lead data center rally for broader confidence; $200B CapEx justified by contract backlog per Dylan Patel.
AWS major Nvidia customer; hosts OpenAI training; receiving Vera Rubin; benefits from CUDA ecosystem expansion; Anthropic expanding to AWS.
Core holding for long-time investor (Chris) from pandemic low; strong performer; maintain position.
Broke out of negative range, +4% rally; market recognizing AWS will not be wiped out by Anthropic; no new news, pure sentiment recovery.
Marvell's biggest customer; selling out of chips; phenomenal deal for Marvell; expected to drive big Marvell quarter; hyperscaler capex driver.

200B capex vs expected 146.6B shocked market; AWS strong but overshadowed; Q1 operating income worse than expected; down 5%+; requires faith in Jassy; holding for Investing Club but hard to own; investments should pay off eventually.
Being beaten by Walmart on web services, by Google on everything; market's already reached verdict; doesn't get respect deserved; reports this week uncertain.
Become bit of a GR battleground; used to roar on earnings now gets faded on ups; if down keeps going lower; greatness lost in shuffle; owned for charitable trust.
Developing own chips but massive Nvidia customer. Hyperscaler demand from Amazon enormous and growing — validates Nvidia ecosystem strength.
Rallied on news of big planned layoffs post-Intel; money flowing back to Mag 7 from shortage plays.
Household name with 20-year history of outperformance via disciplined stock picking; core Magnificent 7 holding despite P/E compression; money will return.
Hyperscaler benefiting from AI data center orders; exposed to OpenAI's ability to fund compute commitments.
Worst performing mega-cap last year but slightly up YTD; holding up better than peers in quality tech selloff.
Dual retail and tech exposure; part of diversified portfolio; sector rotation beneficiary.

Mistaken identity—stock fell 5% in 2025 despite strong AWS re-acceleration (>20% growth), robust retail (double-digit growth), exceptional ad business. Now catching up on repricing fundamental strength.

Up 5% (Mag 7 laggard), mistaken identity; no fundamental problems; AWS re-accelerated >20%, retail double-digit growth, advertising 'total gravy'; $1B Anthropic investment, AI/robotics (Rufus) enhancing retail; clear-ROI spending; Cramer looking 'much bigger gains' 2026.
